bank_purpose: Give students repeated practice with prefixes that signal wrong, bad, false, or against in real academic and everyday words.
teacher_note_on_meaning: Keep the meaning signal visible, but return to real word use so students do not overgeneralize every prefix.

starter_examples.display_prompt: Which prefix makes a real word with behave?
starter_examples.explanation: misbehave is a real word and carries the sense of wrong behaviour.

core_examples.display_prompt: Which prefix makes a real word with information to mean false information?
core_examples.explanation: misinformation is a real word and signals false or misleading information.

core_examples.display_prompt: Which prefix makes a real word with social to mean against social systems or behaviour?
core_examples.explanation: antisocial is a real word in this context.

stretch_examples.display_prompt: Which prefix makes a real word with science to mean false science?
stretch_examples.explanation: pseudoscience is a real academic word and signals false science.

fake.display_prompt: Which prefix makes a real word with kind to mean bad kind?
fake.explanation: None of these create a standard real word with kind in this sense.

near_miss.display_prompt: Which prefixes make real words with trust?
near_miss.explanation: mistrust is standard; antitrust is real but changes the stem and meaning pattern, so use only if taught explicitly.
near_miss.teacher_note: Good arguable discussion if antitrust has not been taught.

arguable.display_prompt: Which prefix best fits social to signal opposition or harmful behaviour?
arguable.explanation: antisocial is the strongest standard form, but discussion may pull in misconduct language instead of a direct word build.
arguable.teacher_note: Use only when discussion time exists.

teacher_note_on_traps: Use fake traps to protect precision, near-miss traps to surface overgeneralization, and arguable traps only when you are ready to name why one answer wins.
sentence_target_bank: His behaviour at lunch was antisocial and rude.
repair_target_bank: The poster shared missinformation about the event.
The word antisocial does not mean anti-friends in every context.
starter_notes: Keep the meaning signal visible beside the prefix.
core_notes: Mix real words with one fake or none-correct round to stop guessing.
stretch_notes: Use academic words like pseudoscience only after the base meaning is secure.
usage_notes: This bank is strongest when students must explain why the form is real, not just identify the prefix.
